title: T-024 — Integration test: shift_aggregator → MV → outbox → 12-REPORTING/13-MAINTENANCE wiring (15-OEE-f)
prompt:
# T-024 — Cross-module integration test

PRD: docs/prd/15-OEE-PRD.md §8.4 (shift aggregation flow), §12.1 (consumer relationships), §12.2 (outbox), D-OEE-2; cross-module 12-REPORTING + 13-MAINTENANCE (D-MNT-3)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
End-to-end vitest exercising the full P1 OEE compute + integration chain: seed oee_snapshots → trigger shift_aggregator_v1 (T-008) → oee_shift_metrics MV populated → outbox row written (T-010 schema) → dispatcher routes to 12-REPORTING cache-invalidate queue + 13-MAINTENANCE MTBF/MTTR feed (D-MNT-3) → both consumer mocks observe the event with correct payload. Closes Auditor A's cross-module wiring gap.

## Pre-flight
All T-006/T-007/T-008/T-010 must be merged. Verify worker test harness from Wave1 T-111/T-112.

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/web/__tests__/integration/oee-shift-to-consumers.test.ts` runs an in-process scenario:
   - Seed two orgs each with 480 oee_snapshots (8h AM shift, per-minute) for line 'LINE-01'.
   - Advance fake-time past shift_end_time + 5 min.
   - Tick the worker registry (T-111) — shift_aggregator_v1 fires for both orgs.
   - Assert oee_shift_metrics has exactly one row per (org, line, shift_date, shift_id); idempotent re-run keeps row count=2.
   - Assert oee_outbox_events has exactly one row per org with event_type='oee.shift.aggregated' and payload matches Zod schema (T-010).
   - Tick the outbox dispatcher (T-010) — both consumer mocks (12-REPORTING cache-bust + 13-MAINTENANCE MTBF/MTTR) receive the event with correct payload exactly once.
   - Assert event_id is unique and idempotent — second dispatcher tick is a no-op (status='delivered').
   - Assert RLS isolation: orgA's outbox row is invisible from orgB session.
2. The test uses real DB (via existing integration test harness) and real worker tick loop, not in-memory fakes for the SQL paths.

## Files
- apps/web/__tests__/integration/oee-shift-to-consumers.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/__tests__/integration/_helpers/oee-fixtures.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given two orgs each seeded with 480 oee_snapshots and fake-time advanced past shift_end_time + 5 min, when the worker registry ticks shift_aggregator_v1, then oee_shift_metrics has exactly one row per (org, line, shift_date, shift_id) — total 2 rows.
2. Given the aggregator completes, when oee_outbox_events is read, then each org has exactly one row with event_type='oee.shift.aggregated' and payload validates against the Zod schema from packages/events/src/oee.ts.
3. Given the outbox dispatcher ticks, when consumer mocks (12-REPORTING + 13-MAINTENANCE) are inspected, then each org's event is delivered to both consumers exactly once with correct payload, and the outbox row status='delivered' (idempotent second tick is a no-op).
4. Given RLS is enforced, when orgB's session attempts to read orgA's outbox row, then 0 rows are returned (V-OEE-ACCESS-3 via app.current_org_id()).

## Test strategy
- RED: vitest integration test asserting all four ACs.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run __tests__/integration/oee-shift-to-consumers`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not stub the SQL paths — use real DB integration harness so MV CONCURRENTLY refresh is exercised.
- Do not skip RLS isolation assertion — it is the cross-cutting invariant.
- Do not assume consumer module implementations exist — use mocks for 12-REPORTING + 13-MAINTENANCE that record received events (consumer modules implement their side in their respective tasks).
- Do not couple the test to specific outbox dispatcher internals — observe behaviour via outbox table state + consumer mock event log.
- Do not skip idempotency assertion — V-OEE-AGG-2 + dispatcher idempotency are both invariants.
